title: Elatostematoides manillensis
taxonomy: Elatostematoides manillensis belongs to the family Celastraceae, a family of flowering plants that are commonly known as the bittersweet or spindle tree family. This plant is a member of the genus Elatostematoides, which is a small group of plants endemic to the Philippines. The species E. manillensis is a type of climber that is found in the tropical forests of the Philippines. The classification of E. manillensis is further supported by its characteristics, such as its simple leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers. Elatostematoides manillensis is an understory plant that thrives in shaded areas with high humidity and moderate temperatures.

This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds.
